title: Implement tower extension field architecture for GF(p^n)
description: Implement recursive tower extension fields for constructing GF(p^n) from smaller fields. This is the algebraic foundation for high-degree extension field arithmetic, following the tower construction pattern used in pairing-based cryptography. ## Architecture Tower extensions build complex fields recursively from simpler ones: ``` GF(p) → GF(p^2) → GF(p^6) → GF(p^12) degree 2 degree 3 degree 2 ``` Each level is an extension of the previous, keeping intermediate values small enough to fit in CPU registers and enabling hardcoded optimal multiplication formulas. ## Core types ```rust /// A quadratic extension: elements are a0 + a1·u where u^2 = non_residue. /// Uses Karatsuba multiplication (3 base multiplications instead of 4). pub struct QuadraticExt { c0: F, c1: F, } /// A cubic extension: elements are a0 + a1·v + a2·v^2. /// Uses Karatsuba-style 6-mul formula (Devegili et al., ePrint 2006/471). pub struct CubicExt { c0: F, c1: F, c2: F, } ``` Both implement `FiniteField`. When the base field `F` is `ConstField + Copy`, the extension is also `ConstField + Copy`. ## Multiplication algorithms ### Karatsuba for degree-2 (QuadraticExt) For `(a0 + a1·u)(b0 + b1·u)`: 1. `v0 = a0 * b0` 2. `v1 = a1 * b1` 3. `v2 = (a0 + a1) * (b0 + b1)` 4. Result: `(v0 + β·v1) + (v2 - v0 - v1)·u` where β is the non-residue 3 base-field multiplications instead of 4 (25% reduction). ### Karatsuba-style for degree-3 (CubicExt) 6 base-field multiplications instead of 9 (33% reduction). Uses the formula from Devegili et al. (ePrint 2006/471), which works generically across all characteristics (unlike Toom-Cook, which requires division by 2, 3, and 6). ## Wide accumulator integration Tower extensions use **lazy/delayed reduction**: intermediate products at each tower level are accumulated in `Wide` types before reducing. This means: - `QuadraticExt>` accumulates in `(u128, u128)` — two wide components - Reduction happens at the Fp level, not at each tower level - The `max_unreduced_additions()` propagates through the tower: outer levels inherit the base field's reduction bound ## Concrete constructions to support | Field | Tower | Use case | |-------|-------|----------| | GF(p^2) | `QuadraticExt>` | Basic extension, complex-number analogy | | GF(p^4) | `QuadraticExt>>` | Double extension | | GF(p^6) | `CubicExt>>` | BLS12-381 intermediate | | GF(p^12) | `QuadraticExt>>>` | BLS12-381 target field | ## Location `crates/gf2-core/src/gfpn/` module: - `ext_config.rs` — `ExtConfig` trait specifying the non-residue β at each tower level - `quadratic.rs` — `QuadraticExt` type and its `FiniteField` implementation - `cubic.rs` — `CubicExt` type and its `FiniteField` implementation (planned) ## Success criteria - `QuadraticExt>` and `CubicExt>` pass the full field axiom test harness (2248b17d). - Nested towers (`QuadraticExt>>>`) pass axiom tests. - Karatsuba and Karatsuba-style multiplication produce identical results to naive polynomial multiplication. - For `QuadraticExt>` with P fitting in 64 bits: multiplication completes in ≤10 base-field multiplications (3 Karatsuba + overhead).
